1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus. To all God's holy people in Christ Jesus at Philippi, together with the overseers and deacons:
May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
I thank my God every time I remember you.
In all my prayers for you, I always pray with joy,
because you have shared with me in spreading the good news, from the first day until now.
I am sure of this: God, who began a good work in you, will keep on doing it until it is finished on the day of Christ Jesus.
It is right for me to feel this way about you, because I hold you in my heart. Whether I am in prison or defending and proving the good news, you all share God's grace with me.
God knows how much I miss you all, with the deep love of Christ Jesus.
And this is my prayer: that your love will keep growing more and more, together with knowledge and good sense,
so that you can choose what is best, and be pure and without fault on the day of Christ.
I pray that you will be filled with the good things that come through Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God.
Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that what has happened to me has actually helped to spread the good news.
It has become clear to the whole palace guard, and to everyone else, that I am in chains because of Christ.
And because of my chains, most of the believers have become more confident in the Lord and more bold to speak God's word without fear.
It is true that some preach Christ because they are jealous and want to compete, but others preach with good hearts.
These last ones do it out of love, knowing that I am here to defend the good news.
The others preach Christ for selfish reasons, not honestly. They think they can make my chains harder to bear.

But what does it matter? The important thing is that, one way or another, whether for the wrong reasons or the right ones, Christ is preached. And because of that I am glad. Yes, and I will keep being glad,
because I know that, through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ, all this will turn out for my deliverance.
I eagerly expect and hope that I will not be ashamed in any way. Instead, I hope that I will be brave enough, now as always, for Christ to be honored in my body, whether I live or die.
For to me, living is Christ, and dying is gain.
But if I go on living in this body, that means I can keep doing fruitful work. So I do not know which to choose!
I am pulled in two directions. I want to leave this life and be with Christ, which is far better.
But it is more important for you that I stay here in this body.
I am sure of this, so I know I will stay. I will go on with all of you, to help you grow and have joy in your faith.
Then, when I come to you again, you will have even more reason to be proud, in Christ Jesus, because of me.

Whatever happens, live in a way that is worthy of the good news of Christ. Then, whether I come and see you or only hear about you, I will know that you stand firm, united in spirit, working side by side for the faith of the good news,
and not frightened in any way by those who oppose you. This is a sign to them that they will be lost, but that you will be saved — and that this is from God.
For God has given you the honor not only of believing in Christ, but also of suffering for him.
You are going through the same struggle you saw me go through, and now hear that I am still going through.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So if you have any encouragement from being joined to Christ, any comfort from his love, any sharing in the Spirit, any tender feelings and mercy,
then make my joy complete by thinking the same way, having the same love, and being one in spirit and purpose.
Do nothing out of selfishness or pride. Instead, be humble, and think of others as better than yourselves.
Each of you should look out not only for your own interests, but also for the interests of others.
Think the way Christ Jesus thought:
Christ was truly God. But he did not hold on to his place as God's equal.
Instead, he gave it up. He took the place of a servant and was born as a human being.
And when he came as a man, he made himself low. He obeyed God all the way to death — even death on a cross.
So God lifted him to the highest place and gave him the name that is above every name,
so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow — in heaven, on earth, and under the earth —
and every tongue should say that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
So then, my dear friends, you have always obeyed — not only when I was with you, but even more now that I am away. So keep working out your salvation with deep respect for God.
For it is God who is working in you, both to want to do what pleases him and to do it.
Do everything without complaining or arguing,
so that you may be innocent and pure, children of God with no fault, in the middle of a twisted and sinful world. Among them you shine like stars in the sky,
as you hold out the word of life. Then on the day of Christ I can be proud that I did not run or work for nothing.
But even if my life is poured out like an offering on the sacrifice that comes from your faith, I am glad, and I rejoice with all of you.
So you too should be glad and rejoice with me.
I hope, in the Lord Jesus, to send Timothy to you soon, so that I may be cheered up when I hear how you are.
I have no one else like him, who truly cares about how you are.
Everyone else is looking after their own interests, not those of Jesus Christ.
But you know that Timothy has proved himself. Like a son with his father, he has served with me in the work of the good news.
So I hope to send him as soon as I see how things go with me.
And I am sure, in the Lord, that I myself will come soon too.
But I think I must send Epaphroditus back to you. He is my brother, my fellow worker, and my fellow soldier. You sent him to take care of my needs.
He has been longing for all of you, and is troubled because you heard that he was sick.
He really was sick, almost to the point of death. But God had mercy on him, and not only on him but also on me, so that I would not have one sorrow after another.
So I am all the more eager to send him, so that when you see him again you will be glad, and I will have less worry.
So welcome him in the Lord with great joy, and honor people like him,
because he almost died for the work of Christ. He risked his life to give me the help you could not give.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Finally, my brothers and sisters, be glad in the Lord. It is no trouble for me to write the same things to you again, and it keeps you safe.
Watch out for those “dogs,” those people who do evil, those who cut the body.
For we are the ones who are truly circumcised — we who worship by the Spirit of God, who are proud of Christ Jesus, and who put no trust in human effort.
Yet I myself could trust in such things. If anyone thinks he has reason to trust in human effort, I have more:
I was circumcised on the eighth day. I am of the people of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew born of Hebrews. As for the law, I was a Pharisee.
I was so eager that I attacked the church. As for being right by the law, no one could find fault in me.
But all the things that were a gain to me, I now count as a loss for the sake of Christ.

More than that, I count everything as a loss compared with the far greater thing of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. For his sake I have lost all things. I count them as garbage, so that I may gain Christ

and be found in him. I do not have a rightness of my own that comes from the law. Instead, I have the rightness that comes through faith in Christ — the rightness that comes from God and is based on faith.
I want to know Christ, and the power that raised him from death, and to share in his sufferings, becoming like him in his death,
so that somehow I may rise from death myself.
I do not mean that I have already reached this, or that I am already perfect. But I keep going, trying to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus took hold of me.
Brothers and sisters, I do not think I have taken hold of it yet. But this one thing I do: I forget what is behind me and reach out for what is ahead.
I keep running toward the goal, to win the prize that God has called me to receive in Christ Jesus.
All of us who are mature should think this way. And if you think differently about something, God will make that clear to you too.
Only let us live up to what we have already reached.
Brothers and sisters, join together in following my example, and watch those who live the way we showed you.
For there are many who live as enemies of the cross of Christ. I have told you about them often, and now I tell you again, even with tears.
They are heading for destruction. Their god is their stomach, and they are proud of what should make them ashamed. Their minds are set on earthly things.
But our home is in heaven. And we are waiting for a Saviour from there, the Lord Jesus Christ.
By the power that lets him bring everything under his control, he will change our weak earthly bodies to be like his own glorious body.
My dear brothers and sisters, I love you and want to see you. You bring me joy and make me proud of you. Continue to be faithful to the Lord.
I plead with Euodia and I plead with Syntyche to agree in the Lord.

For this I make a special request to my friend who has served with me so faithfully: Help these women. They worked hard with me in telling people the gospel, together with Clement and others who worked with me. Their names are written in the book of life.
Always be filled with joy in the Lord. I will say it again. Be filled with joy.
Let your gentleness be known to all men, because the Lord is coming again soon.
Don’t worry about anything, but in everything by prayer and pleading with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God.
Then you will experience God’s peace, which exceeds anything we can understand. His peace will guard your hearts and minds as you live in Christ Jesus.

Brothers and sisters, think about the things that are true and honest, things that are right and pure, and things that are beautiful and good report. Yes, you must think of things that are good and beautiful. If there be any honor, if there be any praise think on these things.
The things you have learned and received from me. The things you have heard and seen me do. You must do the same, then God who gives peace will be with you.
I am very happy and thankful that you have shown again that you think of me. I know that you were thinking of me before, but you could not show it.
I am telling you this, but not because I need anything. I have learned to be happy with what I have.

I know how to live when I am poor and when I have plenty. I have learned the secret of how to live through any kind of situation—when I have enough to eat or when I am hungry, when I have everything I need or when I have nothing.
I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me
But you did the right thing when you helped me when I had trouble and needed help.

You in the congregation of Philippi remember when I first told the Good News there. When I left Macedonia, you were the only church that talked about giving me the help I need. Philippians 416 You also sent me things that I needed more than once when I was in Thessalonica.
Really, it is not that I want to get gifts from you. But I want you to have the benefit that comes from giving.

I have everything I need. I have even more than I need. I have all I need because Epaphroditus brought your gift to me. Your gift is like a sweet-smelling sacrifice offered to God. God accepts that sacrifice and it pleases him.
My God will use his riches to give you everything you need. He will do this through Christ Jesus.
Glory to our God and Father forever and ever. Amen.
Greetings to all the believers in the town of Philippi who belong to Christ Jesus. The friends who are here with me send their greetings.
All the believers here send greetings to you. The people who work for Caesar send special greetings.
I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be gracious to you all.
